data.description:
A single failed Jira mutation (e.g. an unreachable workflow transition) aborts the
entire reconciler pass mid-way, silently skipping all later mutations. The
reconciler should isolate per-mutation failures: apply everything it can, then fail
loud at the END with a non-zero exit and a report of what failed.

## Reproduction Steps

1. Have a local ticket whose mapped Jira status is not reachable via any available
   workflow transition on its Jira issue (e.g. a local `idea` before the Jira
   `IDEA` status/transitions are provisioned).
2. Run a reconcile pass that includes that ticket plus other tickets with valid
   mutations.
3. Observe the pass abort when it reaches the unreachable transition.

## Expected vs Actual

- Expected: the reconciler applies every other (valid) mutation in the pass,
  records the failed one, and at the END exits non-zero with a report naming the
  failed mutation(s) — no valid mutation is silently skipped.
- Actual: `transition_issue_by_name` raises an uncaught `RuntimeError` (no
  transition reaches the target), or the preflight raises `StatusMappingError`,
  aborting the whole pass mid-way; mutations for all subsequent tickets are skipped
  until the next scheduled run.

## Context

This is independent of the `idea` feature (it affects every transition), but it is
the mechanism that makes the `idea → IDEA` create-drops-status convergence and the
"Jira not yet configured" window tolerable instead of pass-aborting — see the
`idea` epic and its "Jira `idea ↔ IDEA` mapping" story. Relevant code: the abort
sites are the preflight in `reconcile.py` (`StatusMappingError`), the per-mutation
error handling in `dispatch_one.py` (only HTTP-400 "illegal transition" currently
degrades to a comment-fallback), and the `RuntimeError` in
`acli.py::transition_issue_by_name` when no transition reaches the target.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A reconcile pass containing one unreachable-transition mutation still applies
      every other valid mutation in that pass (test-verified).
- [ ] The failed mutation is recorded and surfaced in the pass's end-of-run report.
- [ ] The pass exits non-zero when any mutation failed (fail loud), rather than
      exiting 0 or aborting mid-pass.
- [ ] A `RuntimeError` from `transition_issue_by_name` (no transition reaches the
      target) is caught per-mutation and does not abort the pass.
- [ ] The status-mapping preflight does not abort the whole pass for a single
      unmapped/unreachable status.
- [ ] `make lint` and the reconciler test suite pass.
data.title: Reconciler aborts whole pass on one failed Jira transition; should isolate + fail loud at end
